# Test cmd2mcpserver stdin detection
#
# This test verifies that cmd2mcpserver correctly detects programs that use stdin

# Create a program that reads from stdin
-- reader/main.go --
package main

import (
	"bufio"
	"flag"
	"fmt"
	"os"
)

var prefix = flag.String("prefix", "", "prefix to add to each line")

func main() {
	flag.Parse()
	
	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(os.Stdin)
	for scanner.Scan() {
		fmt.Printf("%s%s\n", *prefix, scanner.Text())
	}
}

-- reader/go.mod --
module github.com/test/reader

go 1.21

# Run cmd2mcpserver with tool-def to see if stdin is detected
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -tool-def -source reader reader
stdout '"stdin":\s*{'
stdout '"type":\s*"string"'
stdout '"description":\s*"Optional input data to provide to the command via stdin"'

# Verify that the tool definition includes stdin
stdout '"inputSchema"'
stdout '"properties"'
stdout '"prefix"'

# Create a program that doesn't use stdin
-- noread/main.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
)

var name = flag.String("name", "world", "name to greet")

func main() {
	flag.Parse()
	fmt.Printf("Hello, %s!\n", *name)
}

-- noread/go.mod --
module github.com/test/noread

go 1.21

# Run cmd2mcpserver on the non-stdin program
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -tool-def -source noread noread
! stdout '"stdin"'
stdout '"name"'

# Test dry-run output includes stdin handling
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -dry-run -source reader reader
stdout 'if stdinData, ok := args\["stdin"\]'
stdout 'cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader\(stdinStr\)'

# Test a program that uses file I/O but not stdin
-- fileio/main.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
	"io/ioutil"
	"os"
)

var filename = flag.String("file", "", "file to read")

func main() {
	flag.Parse()

	if *filename == "" {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "Please provide a filename")
		os.Exit(1)
	}

	data, err := ioutil.ReadFile(*filename)
	if err != nil {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error reading file: %v\n", err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}

	fmt.Print(string(data))
}

-- fileio/go.mod --
module github.com/test/fileio

go 1.21

# Verify file I/O program doesn't get stdin parameter
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -tool-def -source fileio fileio
! stdout '"stdin"'
stdout '"file"'
stdout '"description":\s*"file to read"'

# Test a program with multiple I/O operations but no stdin
-- multiio/main.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"net/http"
	"os"
)

var (
	port   = flag.String("port", "8080", "server port")
	debug  = flag.Bool("debug", false, "enable debug logging")
	output = flag.String("output", "", "output file")
)

func main() {
	flag.Parse()

	if *debug {
		log.SetOutput(os.Stderr)
		log.Println("Debug mode enabled")
	}

	if *output != "" {
		file, err := os.Create(*output)
		if err != nil {
			log.Fatal(err)
		}
		defer file.Close()
		file.WriteString("Server started\n")
	}

	http.HandleFunc("/", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		fmt.Fprintf(w, "Hello from server on port %s\n", *port)
	})

	fmt.Printf("Starting server on port %s\n", *port)
	log.Fatal(http.ListenAndServe(":"+*port, nil))
}

-- multiio/go.mod --
module github.com/test/multiio

go 1.21

# Verify complex I/O program without stdin doesn't get stdin parameter
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -tool-def -source multiio multiio
! stdout '"stdin"'
stdout '"port"'
stdout '"debug"'
stdout '"output"'

# Test a program that uses io.Reader but not stdin
-- reader/main.go --
package main

import (
	"bufio"
	"flag"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"strings"
)

var text = flag.String("text", "hello", "text to process")

func processReader(r io.Reader) {
	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(r)
	for scanner.Scan() {
		fmt.Println(scanner.Text())
	}
}

func main() {
	flag.Parse()

	// Use a string reader, not stdin
	reader := strings.NewReader(*text)
	processReader(reader)
}

-- reader/go.mod --
module github.com/test/reader

go 1.21

# Verify io.Reader usage without stdin doesn't trigger stdin parameter
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -tool-def -source reader reader
! stdout '"stdin"'
stdout '"text"'
